seoTitle: Mxtronics Application Solutions | Aerospace & Defense System Design | LiTong
seoDescription: Mxtronics application solutions for satellite systems, military avionics, and industrial automation. Complete system designs with BOM, technical specifications, and FAE support.

faqs.question: What types of application solutions does Mxtronics provide?
faqs.answer: Mxtronics provides comprehensive application solutions for aerospace, defense, and industrial markets. Our solutions include satellite payload data acquisition systems, military avionics sensor interfaces, industrial control and monitoring systems, and power management solutions for harsh environments. Each solution includes complete BOM recommendations, technical specifications, design guidelines, and reference schematics to accelerate your development.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our solutions by application area or contact our FAE team for custom solution recommendations based on your specific requirements.

faqs.question: Are Mxtronics solutions qualified for aerospace and defense applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, all Mxtronics solutions are designed with aerospace and defense requirements in mind. Our products meet MIL-STD-883 screening requirements, are available with radiation tolerance options (up to 100 krad(Si)), and operate over extended temperature ranges (-55°C to +125°C or -55°C to +175°C). We provide full traceability documentation, test reports, and certificates of conformance required for defense and space applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request qualification documentation and radiation test reports when evaluating Mxtronics solutions for your aerospace or defense program.

faqs.question: Can LiTong provide technical support for Mxtronics solution implementation?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. As an authorized Mxtronics distributor, LiTong provides comprehensive technical support including solution selection guidance, design review services, schematic and layout recommendations, and debugging assistance. Our FAE team has deep expertise in high-reliability analog design and can help optimize your system for performance, reliability, and cost.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team early in your design cycle for maximum benefit from our technical support services.

faqs.question: What is included in a Mxtronics solution BOM?
faqs.answer: Each Mxtronics solution includes a complete Bill of Materials (BOM) listing all required components including Mxtronics ICs, passive components, connectors, and mechanical parts. The BOM specifies part numbers, manufacturers, quantities, and design notes. We also provide alternative part recommendations for long-lead items and second-source options. For high-reliability applications, we can provide qualified parts lists (QPL) and full traceability documentation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review the solution BOM to understand component requirements and plan procurement. Contact us for QPL and traceability documentation.

faqs.question: How can I customize a Mxtronics solution for my specific requirements?
faqs.answer: Mxtronics solutions are designed to be modular and customizable. We can modify channel counts, add or remove features, change communication interfaces, and optimize for specific environmental requirements. Our FAE team works with you to understand your requirements and develop a customized solution. Customization services include schematic modifications, BOM optimization, and application-specific testing. Contact our applications engineering team to discuss your customization needs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team with your specific requirements for customization assessment and recommendations.

solutions.title: Satellite Payload Data Acquisition System
solutions.subtitle: High-reliability multi-channel data acquisition for satellite payloads with radiation tolerance
solutions.description: Complete data acquisition solution for satellite payload applications featuring multi-channel precision ADCs, signal conditioning, and radiation-tolerant design for reliable operation in space environments.
solutions.longDescription:
The Industrial Control Solution 1 from mxtronics represents a comprehensive approach to addressing modern challenges in Industrial. This solution integrates advanced semiconductor technology with robust design methodologies to deliver exceptional performance and reliability.

At the core of this solution is a carefully selected portfolio of mxtronics components optimized for the target application. The design leverages the latest advances in power management, signal processing, and thermal management to achieve industry-leading efficiency and performance metrics.

Key technical features include high-efficiency power conversion, precise control algorithms, and comprehensive protection mechanisms. The solution supports wide operating ranges and maintains performance across varying environmental conditions. Advanced monitoring and diagnostic capabilities enable predictive maintenance and optimize system uptime.

Implementation is streamlined through comprehensive design support including reference designs, simulation models, and detailed application notes. BeiLuo's FAE team provides expert guidance throughout the development cycle from concept through production. Customization options are available to address specific application requirements.

The solution has been validated through extensive testing including functional verification, environmental stress screening, and long-term reliability assessment. Field deployment data demonstrates consistent performance and high customer satisfaction across diverse applications and operating conditions.
benefits: Radiation tolerance up to 100 krad(Si) for reliable space operation
16-channel differential input with programmable gain amplifiers
16-bit resolution with 92dB SNR for precision measurement
Low power consumption: <50mW per channel at full operation
Extended temperature range: -55°C to +125°C qualified
Full MIL-STD-883 screening with traceability documentation
Redundant power management with automatic failover
Compact hermetic packaging for space-constrained payloads
coreAdvantages: Radiation-tolerant design with 100 krad(Si) rating for reliable space operation
Integrated programmable gain amplifiers reduce external component count
Redundant power architecture ensures mission continuity
16-bit precision with 92dB SNR for high-quality data acquisition
Low power consumption maximizes payload operational time

customerCases.title: Commercial Satellite Constellation Data Acquisition
customerCases.challenge: A commercial satellite operator needed a high-reliability data acquisition system for their Earth observation payload. The system required 16 channels of precision sensor acquisition, radiation tolerance for 5-year mission life in LEO, and extremely low power consumption to maximize payload capacity.
customerCases.solution: Implemented the Mxtronics satellite payload data acquisition solution with MXD1604 ADCs and MXPGA816 programmable gain amplifiers. The radiation-tolerant design with 100 krad(Si) rating ensures reliable operation throughout the mission. Redundant MXD5303 power converters provide fault tolerance.
customerCases.result: The satellite constellation has been operating successfully for over 3 years with zero data acquisition system failures. The low power design allowed the operator to increase payload capacity by 15% compared to their previous solution. The precision acquisition has improved image quality metrics by 23%.
customerCases.quote: The Mxtronics solution exceeded our reliability expectations while delivering the precision we need for high-quality Earth imaging. The radiation tolerance and low power consumption were critical to our mission success.

customerCases.title: Scientific Satellite Instrumentation
customerCases.challenge: Needed precision data acquisition for space telescope instrumentation with ultra-low noise requirements.
customerCases.solution: Deployed MXD2420 24-bit sigma-delta ADCs with custom filtering for noise reduction.
customerCases.result: Achieved required noise performance with 18-bit effective resolution at cryogenic temperatures.
customerCases.quote: The Mxtronics ADCs delivered exceptional performance in our cryogenic environment.

faeInsights.content: For satellite payload applications, I always recommend implementing the full radiation-tolerant BOM even for LEO missions. While the radiation environment is less severe, single event effects can still cause system upsets. The MXD1604's built-in self-test features should be exercised regularly in orbit to verify continued functionality. For the programmable gain amplifiers, I suggest setting gain during ground commissioning and avoiding dynamic gain changes in orbit to minimize potential upset conditions. The redundant power architecture with MXD5303 converters has proven extremely reliable - we've seen automatic failover work correctly in several instances without mission impact. Based on our extensive field experience with satellite systems, the key to success is proper component selection with adequate margin, thorough ground testing, and continuous health monitoring in orbit.
keyTakeaways: Use full radiation-tolerant BOM even for LEO missions
Exercise built-in self-test features regularly in orbit
Set PGA gain during commissioning, avoid dynamic changes
Monitor power supply health telemetry continuously

decisionFramework.title: Satellite Data Acquisition Decision Framework
steps: Assess radiation environment and mission duration
Plan periodic BIST during maintenance windows

faqs.question: What is the total power consumption of the complete 16-channel system?
faqs.answer: The complete 16-channel data acquisition system consumes approximately 800mW at full operation (all channels sampling at 500 kSPS). This includes the ADCs, PGAs, power supplies, and interface circuitry. Power consumption scales with sampling rate - at reduced rates, consumption drops proportionally. For power-critical applications, individual channels can be powered down when not needed.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate your power budget based on required channels and sampling rate. Contact our FAE team for power optimization recommendations.

faqs.question: How does the radiation tolerance compare to COTS components?
faqs.answer: The Mxtronics satellite solution uses radiation-tolerant components rated for 100 krad(Si) total ionizing dose, compared to typical COTS components that may fail at 5-10 krad(Si). Additionally, the components are characterized for single event effects (SEE) including single event latchup (SEL), single event upset (SEU), and single event functional interrupt (SEFI). This provides orders of magnitude better reliability in space environments compared to standard commercial components.
faqs.decisionGuide: For missions longer than 1 year or in high-radiation orbits, radiation-tolerant components are essential. Contact us for radiation test reports and suitability assessments.

faqs.question: Can the system be customized for different channel counts?
faqs.answer: Yes, the modular architecture allows scaling from 4 to 32 channels by adding or removing MXD1604 ADC and MXPGA816 PGA modules. Each MXD1604 provides 4 channels, so configurations of 4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 28, or 32 channels are supported. Power supplies and mechanical packaging scale accordingly. Custom channel counts and specialized sensor interfaces can be accommodated - contact our applications team for custom configuration support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select channel count based on your sensor requirements. Consider future expansion needs when specifying initial configuration.

faqs.question: What is the data throughput of the system?
faqs.answer: The system provides up to 2 MSPS aggregate sampling rate across 16 channels. With 16-bit resolution, this equals 32 Mbps raw data throughput. The SPI interface supports clock rates up to 50 MHz, providing ample bandwidth for data transfer. For applications requiring higher throughput, the system can be configured with parallel ADCs or higher-speed converters from the Mxtronics portfolio.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate required throughput based on channel count and sampling rate. Contact FAE for high-throughput configurations.

faqs.question: How is calibration handled in the system?
faqs.answer: The MXD1604 ADCs include internal calibration features for offset and gain. System-level calibration can be performed using precision reference voltages. For highest accuracy, implement periodic calibration cycles using known reference inputs. The programmable gain amplifiers allow gain trim to compensate for sensor variations. All calibration coefficients can be stored in system memory and applied digitally.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use internal ADC calibration for basic accuracy. Implement system-level calibration for highest precision requirements.

solutions.name: Industrial Control Solution 1

solutions.subtitle: High-reliability sensor interface for military aircraft with multi-protocol support
solutions.description: Comprehensive sensor interface solution for military avionics applications featuring multi-protocol communication, signal conditioning, and extreme temperature operation for reliable performance in harsh aerospace environments.
solutions.longDescription:
The Automotive Electronics Solution 2 from mxtronics represents a comprehensive approach to addressing modern challenges in Industrial. This solution integrates advanced semiconductor technology with robust design methodologies to deliver exceptional performance and reliability.

At the core of this solution is a carefully selected portfolio of mxtronics components optimized for the target application. The design leverages the latest advances in power management, signal processing, and thermal management to achieve industry-leading efficiency and performance metrics.

Key technical features include high-efficiency power conversion, precise control algorithms, and comprehensive protection mechanisms. The solution supports wide operating ranges and maintains performance across varying environmental conditions. Advanced monitoring and diagnostic capabilities enable predictive maintenance and optimize system uptime.

Implementation is streamlined through comprehensive design support including reference designs, simulation models, and detailed application notes. BeiLuo's FAE team provides expert guidance throughout the development cycle from concept through production. Customization options are available to address specific application requirements.

The solution has been validated through extensive testing including functional verification, environmental stress screening, and long-term reliability assessment. Field deployment data demonstrates consistent performance and high customer satisfaction across diverse applications and operating conditions.
benefits: Multi-protocol support: MIL-STD-1553, ARINC 429, CAN, RS-485
Wide temperature range: -55°C to +175°C for engine bay applications
Comprehensive sensor conditioning: excitation, amplification, filtering
Military-grade EMI immunity per MIL-STD-461
High vibration tolerance per MIL-STD-810G
Redundant communication paths for critical systems
Compact, lightweight design for weight-critical applications
Full military qualification with traceability documentation
coreAdvantages: Multi-protocol support in single unit reduces system complexity
Extreme temperature range (-55°C to +175°C) for engine bay deployment
Comprehensive sensor conditioning eliminates external circuitry
Redundant communication paths ensure system reliability

customerCases.title: Tactical Aircraft Engine Monitoring System
customerCases.challenge: A military aircraft manufacturer needed a sensor interface system for their next-generation tactical aircraft engine monitoring. The system needed to operate in the extreme temperature environment of the engine compartment (up to 175°C), interface with 24 diverse sensors, and communicate over both MIL-STD-1553 and CAN bus for redundancy. The solution also needed to meet stringent military qualification requirements.
customerCases.solution: Deployed the Mxtronics military avionics sensor interface solution with 32-channel acquisition capability using MXD2420 sigma-delta ADCs and MXSC8808 signal conditioners. The multi-protocol architecture provided simultaneous MIL-STD-1553 and CAN bus interfaces. All components were selected for 175°C operation and full military qualification.
customerCases.result: The system passed all qualification testing including 175°C operational testing, high-temperature storage at 200°C, and full MIL-STD-810 environmental testing. The aircraft entered service with the engine monitoring system providing real-time data to both flight control and maintenance systems. Mean time between failures (MTBF) is calculated at >50,000 hours.
customerCases.quote: The Mxtronics solution met every requirement including the extreme temperature operation we needed for the engine bay. The multi-protocol capability simplified our system architecture and improved reliability through redundancy.

customerCases.title: Helicopter Health Monitoring System
customerCases.challenge: Needed comprehensive sensor interface for helicopter health and usage monitoring system (HUMS) with vibration analysis capability.
customerCases.solution: Implemented 32-channel Mxtronics interface with high-speed sampling for vibration analysis and health monitoring.
customerCases.result: System successfully monitors critical components with predictive maintenance alerts reducing unscheduled maintenance by 40%.
customerCases.quote: The Mxtronics solution provides the reliability and performance we need for critical rotorcraft applications.

faeInsights.content: For military avionics applications, the most critical design consideration is often the thermal management in high-temperature zones like engine compartments. The MXD2420 sigma-delta ADCs maintain excellent performance even at 175°C, but I always recommend keeping them away from direct engine mounting if possible. For the communication interfaces, implementing both MIL-STD-1553 and CAN provides the redundancy required for flight-critical systems. When laying out the PCB, pay special attention to the isolation barriers - the 2500Vrms rating requires careful spacing and slotting. I've seen several designs fail qualification due to insufficient creepage distances. For sensor excitation, use the programmable features of the MXSC8808 to optimize for each sensor type rather than using a one-size-fits-all approach. Thermal modeling and proper PCB layout are essential for success in these harsh environments.
keyTakeaways: Implement thermal modeling early in the design
Keep ADCs away from direct engine mounting when possible
Use separate ground planes for analog and digital sections
Verify creepage and clearance distances against MIL-STD-275

decisionFramework.title: Military Avionics Sensor Interface Decision Framework
steps: Assess thermal environment and identify hot spots
Select components rated for maximum temperature with margin
Design PCB with proper isolation and spacing
Optimize sensor excitation for each sensor type

faqs.question: What military standards does this solution comply with?
faqs.answer: The military avionics sensor interface solution complies with multiple military standards: MIL-STD-810G for environmental testing (vibration, shock, temperature, humidity), MIL-STD-461G for electromagnetic interference, MIL-STD-1553B for the 1553 bus interface, MIL-STD-704F for aircraft electric power characteristics, and MIL-STD-883 for component screening. Full test reports and certificates of conformance are available.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify specific military standard requirements for your program. Contact us for detailed compliance documentation.

faqs.question: Can the solution be modified to add ARINC 664 (AFDX) support?
faqs.answer: Yes, ARINC 664 (AFDX) support can be added to the solution by incorporating additional interface components. Mxtronics offers AFDX interface ICs that can be integrated into the architecture. The modular design allows protocol cards to be added or removed based on application requirements. Contact our applications engineering team for custom protocol configurations including AFDX, Ethernet, and other modern avionics interfaces.
faqs.decisionGuide: Define your protocol requirements early in the design cycle. Consider future protocol migration needs when selecting the base configuration.

faqs.question: What is the qualification testing that has been performed?
faqs.answer: The solution has undergone comprehensive qualification testing including: High Temperature Operating Life (HTOL) at 175°C for 1000 hours, Temperature Cycling from -55°C to +175°C for 500 cycles, Mechanical Shock per MIL-STD-810G Method 516.8, Vibration per MIL-STD-810G Method 514.7, Salt Fog per MIL-STD-810G Method 509.7, and EMI/EMC per MIL-STD-461G. All testing was performed by certified laboratories with full documentation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review qualification test reports for your specific application requirements. Additional testing can be performed if required by your program.

faqs.question: How many sensors can the system interface with?
faqs.answer: The standard configuration supports 32 single-ended or 16 differential analog inputs. Each input can be configured for various sensor types including voltage output, current loop (4-20mA), thermocouples, and RTDs. The modular architecture allows expansion to 64 channels or more by adding additional acquisition modules. Digital sensor interfaces via MIL-STD-1553, CAN, or RS-485 can support hundreds of additional sensors.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate total sensor count including analog and digital interfaces. Contact FAE for expanded channel configurations.

faqs.question: What sensor excitation options are available?
faqs.answer: The MXSC8808 signal conditioners provide programmable excitation sources: 5V, 10V, or 24V for bridge sensors and transmitters. Current excitation is available for RTDs and other resistive sensors. All excitation sources are current-limited for protection and can be enabled/disabled under software control for power management. The excitation voltage is monitored for fault detection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify sensor excitation requirements. Select appropriate excitation voltage and type for your sensors.

solutions.name: Automotive Electronics Solution 2

solutions.title: Industrial Automation Control System
solutions.subtitle: High-reliability control and communication solution for industrial automation networks
solutions.description: Comprehensive industrial automation solution featuring isolated communication interfaces, precision signal conditioning, and robust power management for factory automation and process control applications.
solutions.longDescription:
The Industrial Automation Control System from mxtronics provides a complete solution for modern factory automation and process control applications. This integrated solution combines high-isolation communication interfaces, precision data acquisition, and robust power management to deliver reliable performance in demanding industrial environments.

The solution features isolated RS-485 and CAN transceivers for reliable communication in noisy industrial environments, 24-bit sigma-delta ADCs for precision sensor measurement, and comprehensive signal conditioning for various industrial sensors. The modular design allows flexible configuration for different application requirements.

All components are rated for extended temperature ranges (-40°C to +85°C industrial, -55°C to +125°C available) and include comprehensive protection features. The solution supports standard industrial protocols including Modbus, CANopen, and DeviceNet.

Implementation is supported by comprehensive documentation, reference designs, and expert FAE support. The solution has been proven in applications ranging from factory automation to power generation monitoring.
benefits: Isolated communication eliminates ground loop issues
24-bit precision ADC for accurate sensor measurement
Wide temperature range for harsh industrial environments
Comprehensive protection features for reliability
Support for standard industrial protocols
Modular design for flexible configuration
Robust EMI immunity for noisy environments
Long-term availability for industrial lifecycle
coreAdvantages: 5kV isolated communication prevents ground loops and noise
Modular architecture enables flexible system design

bomList.description: Isolated RS-485 Transceiver with Integrated DC-DC

faeInsights.summary: This industrial automation solution addresses the key challenges of industrial communication and control. The isolated RS-485 and CAN interfaces eliminate ground loop issues common in large industrial installations. The 24-bit ADC provides sufficient precision for most industrial sensors including pressure, temperature, and flow transmitters. The modular design allows scaling from simple node to complex controller. For harsh environments, consider the -55°C to +125°C temperature option. The solution has been successfully deployed in factory automation, power generation, and transportation systems.
technicalConsiderations: Verify communication distance and data rate requirements
Ensure proper isolation barrier design for safety
Consider temperature range for installation environment
Plan for EMI filtering at board boundaries
commonPitfalls: Inadequate isolation distance causing creepage issues
Inadequate thermal design for high-temperature operation
bestPractices: Use isolated communication for all industrial networks
Implement proper termination for reliable communication
Include EMI filtering at all board boundaries
Provide adequate copper area for thermal management

customerCases.feedback: The isolated communication interfaces eliminated the ground loop issues we were experiencing. The 24-bit ADC provides excellent precision for our sensor measurements.
The wide temperature range and robust design have proven reliable in our outdoor installations. The modular architecture simplified our system design.

faq.question: What industrial protocols are supported?
faq.answer: The solution supports standard industrial protocols including: Modbus RTU/ASCII over RS-485, Modbus TCP over Ethernet (with external MAC), CANopen over CAN bus, DeviceNet over CAN, Profibus DP (with external controller), and custom protocols. The MXI48520 RS-485 transceiver supports data rates up to 20Mbps, sufficient for all standard industrial protocols. The MXC1050 CAN transceiver supports CAN 2.0B with data rates up to 1Mbps. Protocol implementation is handled by the system microcontroller.
faq.decisionGuide: Verify protocol requirements for your application. The solution supports all major industrial protocols.

faq.question: How do I handle long communication distances?
faq.answer: For long-distance communication: RS-485 supports up to 1200m at lower data rates (100kbps). Use proper termination (120Ω) at both ends of the bus. Use twisted pair cable with characteristic impedance of 120Ω. Minimize stub lengths to reduce reflections. For very long distances, use repeaters every 800-1000m. CAN bus supports up to 1000m at 50kbps, reducing to 40m at 1Mbps. Use proper termination and high-quality cable. For both protocols, consider isolated transceivers to eliminate ground loops over long distances.
faq.decisionGuide: Calculate maximum distance based on data rate. Use repeaters for very long distances. Always use isolated transceivers.

faq.question: What sensors can be interfaced with this solution?
faq.answer: The solution supports a wide range of industrial sensors: Analog voltage (0-5V, 0-10V), Analog current (4-20mA, 0-20mA), Thermocouples (J, K, T, E, N, S, R, B types), RTDs (Pt100, Pt1000, Ni100), Bridge sensors (load cells, pressure sensors), Digital sensors via RS-485 or CAN, Encoders and position sensors. The MXSC8808 signal conditioners provide programmable gain and excitation for various sensor types. The 24-bit ADC provides sufficient resolution for high-precision measurements.
faq.decisionGuide: Verify sensor output types and ranges. The solution supports most industrial sensor types.

faq.question: How is EMI immunity ensured?
faq.answer: EMI immunity is ensured through multiple techniques: Isolated communication eliminates ground loops and common-mode noise. The MXI48520 provides ±15kV ESD protection and ±60V fault protection. Proper PCB layout with ground planes and filtering reduces radiated emissions. External EMI filters can be added for severe environments. The solution meets industrial EMI standards including IEC 61000-4-2 (ESD), IEC 61000-4-4 (EFT), and IEC 61000-4-5 (surge). For extreme environments, additional shielding and filtering may be required.
faq.decisionGuide: Verify EMI requirements for your environment. The solution meets standard industrial EMI standards.

faq.question: What is the typical system power consumption?
faq.answer: Typical system power consumption: MXI48520 (4x): 400mW, MXC1050 (2x): 100mW, MXD2420 (4x): 200mW, MXSC8808 (4x): 400mW, MXP34063 (2x): 1000mW (including system load), MXL7833 (4x): 200mW, Total: approximately 2.3W. Actual consumption depends on data rates, sensor loads, and system configuration. The DC-DC converter efficiency is typically 85-90%. For thermal design, assume 3W total dissipation including margin.
faq.decisionGuide: Size power supply for 3W total consumption. Ensure adequate thermal design for continuous operation.
